headline: COCKROACH JANTA PARTY SIT-IN PROTEST ENTERS DAY 2 AT JANTAR MANTAR, DEMANDS EDUCATION MINISTER'S RESIGNATION
summary: A sit-in protest by the Cockroach Janta Party, led by Abhijit Dipke, is ongoing at Jantar Mantar in New Delhi, with supporters demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. The protest has continued overnight. Over 1,000 police personnel have been deployed in the area.

summary_sources.title: CJP protest in Delhi: Dipke, supporters continue sit-in overnight at Jantar Mantar; urge people to join protest - The Hindu
Cockroach Janta Party: Can it Change Indian Politics? - Frontline

sections.paragraph: Jantar Mantar, an 18th-century observatory built by Maharaja Jai Singh II, has been a prominent site for protests and dharnas in New Delhi since 1993, when it was designated as the official site for such gatherings. This designation came after a large protest by the Bharatiya Kisan Union at the Boat Club in 1988, which led the P.V. Narasimha Rao government to shut down the Boat Club for protests and redirect dissenters to Jantar Mantar Road. Over the decades, countless movements have been born at Jantar Mantar, but many have faded into political oblivion. However, the site's significance as a protest venue has been impacted by a National Green Tribunal order banning protests and dharnas there, effective within four weeks, with protesters to be shifted to Ramlila Maidan. The order has raised concerns among protesters, with some questioning the move. The Delhi government, led by the AAP, has only verbally requested the NGT to review its order, while the NDMC and Delhi police have stopped giving permission for protests at Jantar Mantar.

sources.title: Cockroach Janta Party: Can it Change Indian Politics? - Frontline

sections.paragraph: The Jantar Mantar in New Delhi has become a hub for protests, with activist Sonam Wangchuk recently going on a hunger strike in support of the Cockroach Janta Party's campaign against Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. The protest is related to alleged examination irregularities and a NEET paper leak. Meanwhile, the site itself has a rich history, dating back to 1724 when it was constructed by Maharaja Jai Singh of Jaipur as an observatory with larger and more accurate astronomical instruments. There were five Jantar Mantars built in total across North India, including in Jaipur, Ujjain, Varanasi, and Mathura, with the Delhi location being one of them. The instruments at Jantar Mantar were designed for astronomical observations, but due to the surrounding tall buildings, accurate observations can no longer be made from this location. The site is now more notable for its role in hosting protests and gatherings, such as the recent hunger strike, rather than its original purpose as an astronomical observatory.

sources.title: Jantar Mantar turns hunger strike junction - The Hindu

sections.paragraph: Jantar Mantar, a historic site in New Delhi, has been a prominent location for protests and dharnas since 1993, when it was designated as the official site for such gatherings. The site's proximity to Parliament House, yet not too close, made it a convenient location for protesters. However, recent developments have led to a shift in protest locations, with the National Green Tribunal (NGT) ordering the relocation of protests to the outer periphery of Lutyen's Delhi, citing noise pollution and health concerns. Protesters argue that this move is an attempt to curtail their right to speech and freedom of expression, and that the grounds for the shift are untenable. The relocation to Ramlila Maidan has also raised concerns among protesters, who point out that the new location lacks shade and would expose them to harsh weather conditions. The shift has been met with resistance from protesters, who are concerned about the impact on their ability to express themselves and bring attention to their causes.

sources.title: CJP protest in Delhi: Dipke, supporters continue sit-in overnight at Jantar Mantar; urge people to join protest - The Hindu

sections.paragraph: The Cockroach Janta Party has been planning a protest at Jantar Mantar, with over 1,000 police personnel deployed in New Delhi in anticipation. The protest, which has been largely publicized through social media, is demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over examination-related lapses. The party's supporters, including school and college students, as well as young professionals, have gathered at the venue, chanting slogans and displaying banners. The protest has drawn attention from various political leaders, including Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Sanjay Raut, who shared a message from former Maharashtra Chief Minister Uddhav Thackeray expressing support for the protesters. The Cockroach Janta Party's founder, Abhijeet Dipke, has arrived in Delhi and is seeking permission for the protest, while the police are closely monitoring the situation and making security arrangements. The protest's impact and potential to make a political difference are being debated, with some viewing it as a significant moment in Indian politics.
